chunks.text: GSI(L) LOCAL COMMANDS GSI(L) NAME gsi - X-windows based graphical tool for viewing, measur- ing, or printing plots of the results of an Hspice simula- tion. SYNOPSIS gsi [-a] [input_fi

mula- tion. SYNOPSIS gsi [-a] [input_file_name] DESCRIPTION GSI is an X-windows based graphical user interface to Hspice. From within GSI, the user can run Hspice and con- currently review the simulation and wavef

on- currently review the simulation and waveforms. GSI supports interactive curve measurement. GSI is licensed by a floating token and requires the license manager, metaserver, to be running. Hspice creates the necessary

e running. Hspice creates the necessary graph data files with the addi- tion of a binary file while "POST = 2" creates a portable ASCII file. The Hspice default is to store all the nodal voltages, the current thro

re all the nodal voltages, the current through the power supplies, and any output variables referenced in .PRINT, .PLOT, .PROBE, .GRAPH, or .MEASURE statements. Output can be limited to just the output vari- ables by the a

mited to just the output vari- ables by the addition of the .OPTION PROBE statement. Output file size can be limited by storing only the points specified on the .TRAN statement by adding the .OPTION INTERP statement. COM
by adding the .OPTION INTERP statement. COMMAND OPTIONS -a Auto Graph. Automatically graph the last named file that was viewed in a prior session. Upon startup, the Graph display immediately
startup, the Graph display immediately appears, containing the panels, nodes, and views that were saved. input_file_name This file may be design_name or
may be design_name or design_name.analysis. If no name, or a partial name is given, GSI will try to create an input file name. If no input_file_name is given, GSI by
o input_file_name is given, GSI by default picks the last valid graph data file used in the current directory. If input_file_name has no analysis suffix, GSI appends a standard tran- si
, GSI appends a standard tran- sient analysis suffix ( .tr0). Examples: gsi - rereads last file viewed or prompts for new file gsi aa - reads file aa.tr0 gsi aa.tr0 - reads a
ds file aa.tr0 gsi aa.tr0 - reads aa.tr0 gsi aa.sw2 - reads aa.sw2 META-SOFTWARE Last change: 1/5/94 1 GSI(L) LOCAL COMMANDS GSI(L) CONFIGU

OCAL COMMANDS GSI(L) CONFIGURATION FILES Configuration files describe the available terminals and hardcopy devices. A configuration file contains: -> one terminal setup block describing terminal charac-

terminal setup block describing terminal charac- teristics -> 1-10 plot setup blocks describing hardcopy and queue characteristics -> one reference to a default plot setup -> options for running Hspic

plot setup -> options for running Hspice, HSPLOT, and GSI The meta.cfg file stores design-independent configuration data such as the default printer and printer selection. GSI looks first in your login (~) directory for

I looks first in your login (~) directory for meta.cfg. If it does not find one, it looks in the install directory. The meta.cfg file is built during installation by running "setup_meta" and selecting "install hspice".

"setup_meta" and selecting "install hspice". The design-level configuration file, .cfg, resides in the design directory. It contains Hspice, HSPLOT, and GSI setups applicable to a specific design or program ses- sion.

to a specific design or program ses- sion. For shared variables, such as "Memory", "backup", and "ddlpath", design_name.cfg overrides the meta.cfg files. CUSTOMIZING GSI Preferences may be set in an X defaults

Preferences may be set in an X defaults file named gsi.Xdefaults. Default values for such window features as fonts, colors, window size and position can be set by the file "install_directory/platform/defaults/gsi.

file "install_directory/platform/defaults/gsi.Xdefaults." For example, release 96 on a sun4 holds the file at 96/sun4/defaults/gsi.Xdefaults. The meta.cfg file specifies printers and terminals. See the Hspice User'

rinters and terminals. See the Hspice User's Manual, Vol. 1, for a complete discussion of the meta.cfg file. META WINDOW The "Meta Window" allows the user to control file access and specify which data is to be displayed in th
d specify which data is to be displayed in the Graph Window. FILE Allows the user to open and close graph data files. Hspice Allows the user to run Hspice from GSI. This menu allows the u
from GSI. This menu allows the user to select input and output files, and to edit input files before submitting them to Hspice without leaving GSI. The user can select the Hspice
The user can select the Hspice version number (from /usr/meta/96/bin/versions). The user can specify META-SOFTWARE Last change: 1/5/94 2 GSI(L)
2 GSI(L) LOCAL COMMANDS GSI(L) the graph revision number which Hspice uses to start numbering output files. The memory size can be set
files. The memory size can be set within this window (not necessary for H93a and later). USER Allows the user to initiate any of the commands in the meta.cfg file. QUIT Quits GSI.
the meta.cfg file. QUIT Quits GSI. CLOSE and ACTIVE Select the files that are plotted on the Graph Window. SETUP and DRAW Controls the curves to be drawn in the Graph Win-
trols the curves to be drawn in the Graph Win- dow. "Curves" is a list of nodes to be selected by mouse-clicks that are drawn as curves in the Graph Window. TYPES Lists the types of nodes
Window. TYPES Lists the types of nodes to be displayed. FILTER Allows the nodes to be displayed by pattern match- ing with standard unix wild cards. EXPRESSION A mathematical function of the no
N A mathematical function of the nodes that can be displayed on the Graph Window. SUBCIRCUIT Allows the user to see a subcircuit with its full name or its subcircuit tree name. GR
name or its subcircuit tree name. GRAPH WINDOW The Graph Window displays graphs of the data. SETUP Specifies colors, symbols, and line styles for the curves to be drawn. TITLE Allows a default tit
to be drawn. TITLE Allows a default title for a panel, based on the name of the input file. PANELS Controls the number of panels on which curves can be drawn. MEASUREMENT Allow
be drawn. MEASUREMENT Allows the user to do interactive measurement of the curves. Two forms are available. In PARALLEL format, measurements occur along one curve. After an anc
cur along one curve. After an anchor line is set, measurement can be taken between the x and y points for the curve drawn, META-SOFTWARE Last change: 1/5/94 3 GSI(L)
/5/94 3 GSI(L) LOCAL COMMANDS GSI(L) the first and second derivatives, the delta x and y positions between the anchor and the pointer
ween the anchor and the pointer line, the slope between two points on the curve, and the x intercept. In CURVE-TO-CURVE format, these same measurements can be taken between one c
urements can be taken between one curve and all the remaining curves drawn. PRINT A simple print tool. UPDATE Allows the user to run Hspice in two manners. The RERUN UPDATE displays the results of an
RERUN UPDATE displays the results of an Hspice run that has completed. It redraws all affected curves. The second manner of update allows the curves to be redrawn as the data is calcul
curves to be redrawn as the data is calculated by the still-running Hspice simulation. The curves are updated on-the-fly as the user clicks the mouse button. This manner is useful for
mouse button. This manner is useful for Hspice runs that take a long time to complete. FILES /usr/meta/bin/versions gsi.Xdefaults GSI Input files: *.cfg design configuration all Hspice grap
cfg design configuration all Hspice graph data (Note: # is run or alter number) *.tr# graph data - transient *.mt# graph data - transient .measure output *.sw# graph data - DC
re output *.sw# graph data - DC *.ms# graph data - DC .measure output *.ac# graph data - AC *.ma# graph data - AC .measure output *.gr# hardcopy data from meta.cfg PRTDEFAULT

*.gr# hardcopy data from meta.cfg PRTDEFAULT GSI Output: *.gr# hardcopy data from Print menu NOTES * GSI is limited to 6000 nodes, including probes. * GSI is limited to a default of 100,000 data points (tran points)

a default of 100,000 data points (tran points) per curve. If you need to run a longer simulation, increase the MAXPOINTS in your meta.cfg file. * GSI is limited to 500 display curves. This may be a prob- lem with

play curves. This may be a prob- lem with Monte Carlo or data sweeps, as each node is saved for each sweep as a curve (ie. 20 nodes x 25 sweeps = 500 curves). SEE ALSO hsplot hspice ".GRAPH" metaserver metalicense metastat
pice ".GRAPH" metaserver metalicense metastat META-SOFTWARE Last change: 1/5/94 4
